exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete using the words in the box
exercises.title: A: Complete using the words in the box
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences using the words in the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences using the words in the box.

items.sentence: The island has a ...... climate, so tourists visit all year.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is not extreme, so the correct answer is "mild".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The water in the lake is ...... today!

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is very cold, so the correct answer is "freezing".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Some rare birds may become ...... if we don't protect them.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no longer exist, so the correct answer is "extinct".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Plastic pollution is a ...... issue, not just a local one.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is worldwide, so the correct answer is "global".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "global" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Plastic pollution is a ...... issue, not just a local one.".

items.sentence: We saw several ...... horses running across the hills.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is not tame, so the correct answer is "wild".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

items.sentence: The ...... council wants to plant more trees in the town.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is in the area, so the correct answer is "local".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

exercises.title: B: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The road was dangerous because it was very ...... (FOG).

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from FOG, so the correct answer is "foggy".

wrongOptions.fog: The root FOG cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "foggy".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of FOG that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: FOG → foggy: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "foggy" is the word-formation answer from the root FOG in this context.

items.sentence: A large fire can be extremely ...... (DESTROY).

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from DESTROY, so the correct answer is "destructive".

wrongOptions.destroy: The root DESTROY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "destructive".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DESTROY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: DESTROY → destructive: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "destructive" is the word-formation answer from the root DESTROY in this context.

items.sentence: It is ...... (NATURE) for animals to look for food.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from NATURE, so the correct answer is "natural".

wrongOptions.nature: The root NATURE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "natural".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of NATURE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: NATURE → natural: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "natural" is the word-formation answer from the root NATURE in this context.

items.sentence: Water ...... (POLLUTE) is killing fish in the river.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from POLLUTE, so the correct answer is "pollution".

wrongOptions.pollute: The root POLLUTE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "pollution".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of POLLUTE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: POLLUTE → pollution: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "pollution" is the word-formation answer from the root POLLUTE in this context.

items.sentence: My grandfather enjoys ...... (GARDEN) at the weekend.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is activity from GARDEN, so the correct answer is "gardening".

wrongOptions.garden: The root GARDEN cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "gardening".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of GARDEN that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: GARDEN → gardening: Use the -ing form for an activity or gerund.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "gardening" is the word-formation answer from the root GARDEN in this context.

items.sentence: Nobody knows the exact ...... (DEEP) of that cave.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from DEEP, so the correct answer is "depth".

wrongOptions.deep: The root DEEP cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "depth".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DEEP that fits grammatically, not just the root.

explanationContent.wordFormation: DEEP → depth: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "depth" is the word-formation answer from the root DEEP in this context.

wrongOptions.circle: The root CIRCLE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "circular".

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
No more than twenty people can enter the cave. **most**
Twenty people ...... can enter the cave.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is maximum, so the correct answer is "at most".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "at most", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
In the story, the spaceship explodes near the moon. **up**
In the story, the spaceship ...... near the moon.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is explode, so the correct answer is "blows up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "blows up", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
There are unusual plants on the lowest part of the valley. **bottom**
There are unusual plants ...... the valley.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is lowest part, so the correct answer is "at the bottom of".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "at the bottom of", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
The candle stopped burning before midnight. **out**
The candle ...... before midnight.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is stopped burning, so the correct answer is "went out".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "went out", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
Can you help me hang this wildlife poster? **up**
Can you help me ...... this wildlife poster?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is display, so the correct answer is "put up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "put up", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "put up" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence:
We should increase support for recycling in schools. **build**
We should ...... support for recycling in schools.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is increase gradually, so the correct answer is "build up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "build up", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The rainforest contains millions of different ...... of plants and insects.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is types of living things, so the correct answer is "species".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Rising sea ...... may affect many coastal cities.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ocean height, so the correct answer is "levels".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "levels" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Rising sea ...... may affect many coastal cities.".

items.sentence: Everyone should help protect ...... planet.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is possessive, so the correct answer is "our".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

items.sentence: The earthquake was 6.8 on the Richter ......

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is measurement, so the correct answer is "scale".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "scale" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "The earthquake was 6.8 on the Richter ......".

items.sentence: The volcano ...... after many years of silence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is volcano action, so the correct answer is "erupted".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Several animals are now ...... because their habitats disappeared.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no longer exist, so the correct answer is "extinct".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
